summary: sam-package exfiltrates browser cookies, storage, and DOM to attacker webhook on import
details: index.js executes top-level code on require/import that collects navigator.userAgent, window.location.href, document.cookie, localStorage, sessionStorage, document.referrer, window.__TINES_CONFIG__, window.__APP_CONFIG__, window.parent.location.href, and document.body.innerText, then POSTs the JSON payload to a hardcoded webhookrelay.com webhook (https://bin.webhookrelay.com/v1/webhooks/f982048d-7436-4b00-b508-89f5eb657645) with mode: 'no-cors'. The README only advertises a benign greet(name) helper — the data collection and exfiltration are entirely undocumented. The destination is hardcoded with no user configuration, and the explicit references to __TINES_CONFIG__ and window.parent.location.href indicate targeting of Tines story/embedded browser contexts. Any consumer that imports this package in a browser context silently leaks session cookies, storage tokens, parent-frame URLs, and DOM contents to the attacker-controlled webhook.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
